nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosenbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans.
German: habitational name from any of numerous places so named (see   Rose   and   Bach  ).

variants.name: Roser
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew (see Rose 1), with the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant, or an occupational name for a rose grower, or a nickname for a lover of roses.
German (Röser): habitational name for someone from any of the places called Rös, Roes, or Rösa in Bavaria, Rhineland, and Saxony.
German (Röser): variant of Rösser (see Rosser 3).

variants.name: Roseman
meanings: English (Middlesex): from the Middle English female personal name Rosamunde (ancient Germanic Rosmund, from hros ‘horse’ + mund ‘protection’). The personal name was widely understood in the Middle Ages as Latin rosa munda ‘pure rose’ and therefore as an epithet of the Virgin Mary.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or name adopted by the husband of a woman bearing the Yiddish personal name Royze (see Rose 9).
Americanized form of German Rosemann or Rosmann (see Rossmann ).

variants.name: Roos
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Reisen
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): possibly an artificial name from northeastern Yiddish reyzn ‘roses’ (standard Yiddish royzn); see Rose 9.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Rosado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Carlos, Rafael, Angel, Manuel, Francisco, Ramon, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish: nickname for someone with a notably pink and white complexion, from Spanish rosado ‘pink’, Late Latin rosatus, a derivative of rosa (see Rose ).

variants.name: Roesel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto.
German (Rösel): diminutive of Rose .

variants.name: Rosette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Arturo, Cirilo, Consuelo, Cristina, Eloy, Eufrosina, Felipe, Jesus, Jorge, Manuel.

French: from a pet form of the female personal name   Rose   6 ‘rose’, or a habitational name from any of several places called (La) Rosette, e.g. in Yonne, denoting a place where roses grow. This surname is also found in the West Indies and Mauritius.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Roseth  .

variants.name: Rozsa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Sandor, Tibor, Zoltan, Zsuzsanna, Balint, Gabor, Gyula, Imre, Istvan, Katalin, Laszlo, Miklos.  German Armin, Erwin, Theodor.

Hungarian (Rózsa): from the female personal name Rózsa, from the vocabulary word rózsa ‘rose’. In some cases the surname might derive from an old male personal name of the same form. Compare Roja 2 and Rose 1.

variants.name: Roese
meanings: German (Röse):
variant of   Rose  .
possibly also a habitational name from a place called Rösa near Bitterfeld.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose was born about 1481, in Suffolk, England. She married Tristram Carfinkle about 1501, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose est née environ 1481, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Tristram Carfinkle environ 1501, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose wurde um 1481 in Suffolk, England geboren. Sie hat um 1501 in Suffolk, England Tristram Carfinkle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose è nata il circa 1481 nel Suffolk, England. Lei sposato Tristram Carfinkle circa 1501, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Pembroke Castle, Pembrokeshire, Wales: ウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロク カストル
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Roseは約1481年にイギリススフォルクで生まれた。約1501年にイギリススフォルクでTristram Carfinkleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose(은)는 대략 1481년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1501년 영국 수폴크에서 Tristram Carfinkle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose nasceu aproximadamente 1481, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Tristram Carfinkle aproximadamente 1501, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose родилась около 1481 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Tristram Carfinkle около 1501 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rose nació aproximadamente 1481, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Tristram Carfinkle aproximadamente 1501, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
